questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Direct/Indirect speech. "A certain king has ordered me to get him three of the golden apples," said the boy.
options: The boy said that a certain king ordered him to get him three of the golden apples.
The boy said that a certain king had ordered him to get him three of the golden apples.
The boy said that a certain king had ordered me to get him three of the golden apples.
The boy said that a certain king has ordered him to get him three of the golden apples.
questions.explanation: The present perfect tense (has ordered) should change to the past perfect (had ordered), while converting direct to indirect speech. Additionally, first person objective pronoun 'me' should be converted to the third person objective pronoun 'him'. B is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Detailed briefings, assignment of a buddy and regular sessions with the manager can go a long way in smoothing the transition. Q. A new job can be challenging for most people. R. A broad way organisations can make it easier is by having the right kind of onboarding processes in place. S. But organisations can make it easier for new employees to settle in.
questions.explanation: QS is a pair: Q talks about challenge; S talks about making it easier. SR is also a pair: S talks about organisations; R talks broad solution to it and diving into specifics as discussed in P. RP is a pair; P mentions some of the right kind of onboarding processes. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Cinema plays a major role in our social as well as personal life. Q. As it is the cheapest source of entertainment and amusement available in the market, people spend huge amount of time and money on it. R. It has the power to influence the thinking of people. S. However, of late, it's influencing people and society in general, in a negative way.
questions.explanation: Statement P has to be the opening statement, since all other statements have pronoun(s) in them. PR is a pair (It in R is a reference to Cinema in P). Only option A can satisfy both the criteria; hence, (a) is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Select the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option A is spelt incorrectly. The correct spelling is 'immigrants'. It denotes a person who comes to live permanently in a foreign country.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Select the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option B is spelt incorrectly. The correct spelling is 'dignity'. It means the state or quality of being worthy of honour or respect.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the words similar in meaning to the word given. ARRAIGN
questions.explanation: Arraign (verb) - find fault with or censure; clearly the answer is option a). Reign - hold royal office.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the words similar in meaning to the word given. SPARTAN
questions.explanation: Spartan (adj.) - showing or characterized by austerity or a lack of comfort or luxury; 'austere' has a similar meaning; this makes option c) the correct answer. Platoon - a small group of about ten or twelve soldiers, with a lieutenant in charge of it.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Forlorn
questions.explanation: Forlorn - Pitifully sad and abandoned or lonely. 'Happy' can be its antonym. Harangue - A lengthy and aggressive speech. D is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Ingrate
questions.explanation: Ingrate - Ungrateful. So, 'grateful' can be its antonym. Oblivious - Not aware of or concerned about what is happening around one. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. (Of behaviour or ability) developed at an earlier age than is usual or expected.
questions.explanation: Unequivocal- unambiguous, unmistakable; straightforward. Duplicitous- deceptive in words or action. Precocious- having developed certain abilities or inclinations at an earlier age than is usual or expected. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. One who makes an official examination of accounts
questions.explanation: An auditor is a person authorized to review and verify the accuracy of financial records and ensure that companies comply with tax laws. Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, one part of the sentence may or may not have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If the sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. Private cars are (1)/ supposedly more reliably (2)/ and allow for more flexible work schedules. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The error is in part 2. An adjective modifies a noun correctly. In part 2, the adjective 'reliable' should replace the adverb 'reliably' to correctly modify the noun 'cars'. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, one part of the sentence may or may not have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If the sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. It's no secret that (1)/ Google gather up large (2)/ amounts of your data based on your search history. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The error is in part 2. There is an error of subject-verb-agreement. The singular noun ('Google' in this case) requires a singular verb ('gathers' in this case) to be grammatically correct; 'gather' (plural) should be replaced with 'gathers'. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. She was asking for me.
options: I was being asked for by her.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in the past continuous tense. The rule for changing the voice of a present continuous tense is as follows: Active voice: Subject + was/were + present participle + object → (She + was + asking for + me). Some verbs have prepositions attached to them. We keep the preposition while changing the voice. Passive voice: Object + is/am/are + being + past participle + by + subject → (I + was + being+ asked for + by + her) Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. ___________ to many of us, we actually have a sixth digit - for some it's an extra limb: our mobile phones.
questions.explanation: The author tells us about how important mobile phones have become; they act as an extra limb to us; the usage of 'actually' indicates that the author wants us to know something we are not aware of and is surprising. 'Unbeknownst' (unknown) correctly fits in the given blank and provides the sentence with its intended meaning. Hence, option b) is the answer. 'Proficient' never takes the preposition 'to' with it; hence, option a) is eliminated. The blank requires an adjective to qualify the pronoun 'us'; 'theologian' (one who studies theology, especially Christian) is a noun and hence, option c) is eliminated. 'Tortious' though is an adjective, does not contribute in making the given sentence meaningful; it means 'wrongful'; option d) is eliminated too.

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. While ensuring that popular morality does not castigate minorities, The Indian Constitution provides spaces for change; it is not a hammer to beat people with, but an ideal that allows for steady, __________ evolution.
questions.explanation: The sentence above states one of the features of the Indian Constitution; the blank requires an adjective that can qualify the noun 'evolution'. The words 'feed' and 'birth' are nouns/verbs; hence, do fit grammatically; options a) and d) thus can be eliminated. 'Biotic' means 'relating to or resulting from living organisms'; it clearly fails to fit in the given context; hence, option b) is eliminated too. 'Organic' which means 'Denoting or characterized by a harmonious relationship between the elements of a whole' is in line with the additional adjective 'steady' is the right word for the blank (it will refer to the ordered or systematic evolution that the constitution allows); hence, option c) is the right answer.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the Idiom/Phrase underlined. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase and mark it. work your fingers to the bone
questions.explanation: "Work your fingers to the bone" means to work extremely hard

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the Idiom/Phrase underlined. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase and mark it. come hell or water.
options: To be enraged and show it
questions.explanation: 'Come hell or water' means any difficult situation.

questions.question: What is the reason for the wage gap?
options: Higher representation of women in sectors where their work is undervalued
Large number of leaves taken by women employees
Presence of stringent laws favouring women employees
questions.explanation: From the second paragraph of the passage "a higher representation of women in sectors where their work is undervalued results in a gender pay gap" it is clear that the correct option is (b). More number of women doing undervalued work is the reason for the wage gap.

questions.question: What are the effective measures that were adopted to lower the wage gap?
questions.explanation: From the starting sentence of the third paragraph "Strong labour market institutions and policies such as collective bargaining and minimum wages lowered the pay gap", it can be inferred that both the options (a) and (c) are correct while the option (b) is not at all mentioned in the passage. So the correct answer is (d).

questions.question: Compare the intensity of wage gap in India to that of South Korea?
options: South Korea's wage gap is better than that of India's by 7%
India's wage gap is worse than that of South Korea's by 7%
South Korea's wage gap is worse than that of India's by 7%
South Korea's wage gap is worse than that of India's by 14%
questions.explanation: From the sentences, "Among major economies, only South Korea fared worse than India, with a gap of 37 per cent" and "India had among the worst levels of gender wage disparity - men earning more than women in similar jobs - with the gap exceeding 30 per cent", we can infer that the wage gap in India is 30% and that in South Korea is 37%. So the wage gap in South Korea is worse than that of India by 7%.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements emphasizes the intensity of wage gap in India?
options: The share of women among wage earners was the lowest in South Asia
In India, the wage gap is almost 30%
In India, women formed 60 per cent of the lowest paid wage labour, but only 15 per cent of the highest wage-earners
Women are poorly represented in the top-bracket of wage earners
questions.explanation: The options (a) and (d) are correct as per the passage but the third one is the statement which clearly emphasizes the wage disparity in India. 60% of the lowest paid are women while only 15% of the highly paid are women. So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: Which country had the lowest wage disparity and what is the percentage of wage gap there?
questions.explanation: It is very easy to find out from the passage that Singapore has the lowest wage gap. The sentence which states this is "Singapore had among the lowest, at 3 per cent". Hence the correct answer is option d.

questions.question: In the below questions, a part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose 'No Improvement' option. I am not here to (carry out) your orders.
questions.explanation: Option C is the right answer, as the sentence needs no improvement. Options A, B and D are eliminated.

questions.question: In the below questions, a part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose 'No Improvement' option. Our old school building is being (torn down).
questions.explanation: Option B is the right answer, 'torn down' means to demolish. Hence the sentence needs no improvement.
